blocks.content: Saturday practice. Coach Briggs was running us through combo routes — the kind where the QB and receiver have to make the same read at the same time off the same coverage. Either you're both right or neither of you is.

blocks.speaker: Coach Briggs
blocks.content: This is a trust drill. I'm not looking for the perfect route. I'm looking for two people making the same choice at the same time. Go.
Jonathan and I looked at each other for about half a second. Then we ran it.
Clean. The read happened before the snap even. We both felt the corner sitting back and we both adjusted — him at the line, me at the break — without saying anything.

blocks.speaker: Coach Briggs
blocks.content: That's it. That's what I'm asking for. Do that again.
We ran it again. And again. Every rep was the same — not perfect, but real. The kind of football that happens when two people stop performing and just play.
I glanced at the bleachers at one point. Stacy was there. Same notebook. She was watching the coverage adjustments, marking something.
Jonathan did not glance at the bleachers. He was completely focused. This was the version of him I ran with — the one who loved the work itself, separate from everything around it.
After the session Coach kept me for a minute.

blocks.speaker: Coach Briggs
blocks.content: Jenkins. Whatever shifted this week — keep it. That's the player I've been waiting for.

blocks.prompt: Coach Briggs says that's the player he's been waiting for.

options.label: "I know what shifted." You're going to keep it.
Nod. Let it mean something without performing it.
